data.title: Plaza Retail REIT reports Q2 2025 financial results: Revenues up 3.6% to $31,791k, NOI up 3.8% to $19,098k, profit up 419.2% to $12,653k. FFO increased 5.3% to $11,187k, FFO per unit up 5.3% to $0.100, AFFO decreased 15.8% to $7,511k, AFFO per unit down 16.3% to $0.067. Same-asset NOI grew by 1.5%.业
data.description: Plaza Retail REIT revealed its financial results for the first half of 2025, with revenues reaching $31.8 million in the second quarter, up 3.6% from the same period in 2024. Net operating income increased to $19.1 million, a 3.8% rise from the previous year. The REIT's profit and total comprehensive income also surged to $12.7 million, compared to $2.4 million in the same period last year. Additionally, the REIT accomplished notable milestones, including property conversions and ownership expansions, improving its committed occupancy to 98%. Plaza Retail REIT increased its per unit FFO by 5.3%, achieved a 1.5% growth in same-property NOI, and registered a 14.8% blended leasing spread. The REIT's profit and total comprehensive income for the six months ended June 30, 2025, reached $22.0 million, up from $11.9 million in the prior year. These positive financial results were supported by increased revenue and operational efficiencies across their portfolio. Plaza Retail REIT remains focused on strategic enhancements and developments within its properties to drive long-term value for shareholders.

data.title: Guardian Capital Group (TSX: GCG; GCG.A) reports $90M net revenue, $55M net earnings in Q2 2025, with $164.1B total client assets.
data.description: The article discusses financial results for a company as of June 30, 2025. Total client assets were reported at $164.1 billion, a decrease from $169.0 billion on December 31, 2024, and a significant rise from $58.6 billion on June 30, 2024. Net revenue for the current quarter was $90.0 million, up from $64.2 million in the previous year. Operating earnings and EBITDA were $6.1 million and $14.9 million, respectively, down from $14.3 million and $21.4 million in the same quarter last year. Net gains in the current quarter were $55.0 million, a stark improvement from net losses of $39.2 million in the previous year. Net earnings attributable to shareholders were $55.2 million this quarter, compared to a net loss of $23.1 million in the same period last year. Adjusted cash flow from operations was $10.7 million this quarter, down from $13.9 million in the comparable period. Shareholders' equity as of June 30, 2025, was $1,323 million, and Guardian's Securities net value was $1,248 million. The company declared a dividend of $0.39 per share, payable on October 17, 2025.

data.title: Ironclad appoints former executives from top companies for key roles as they hit 2B contracts processed.
data.description: Ironclad, a prominent AI-powered contract lifecycle management platform, has recently exceeded the milestone of processing over 2 billion contracts. The company made significant announcements in San Francisco on August 4, 2025, introducing key appointments. These include the hiring of a new Chief Technology Officer (CTO), Chief Strategy Officer, and Senior Vice President (SVP) of CAE Sales. The expansion of Ironclad's leadership team comes as a strategic move to further strengthen its position in the market.

data.title: Flutter Entertainment PLC reported Q2 revenue of $4.19 billion, beating estimates, with 15.98M monthly players. US business, including FanDuel, saw significant growth.
data.description: Leading sports betting operator, Flutter Entertainment PLC (NYSE: FLUT), released its second-quarter financial results post-market close. The company reported a record revenue of $4.19 billion for the quarter, marking a significant 16% increase compared to the previous year. This exceeded the $4.06 billion revenue estimate from analysts. Furthermore, Flutter reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.95, surpassing the consensus estimate of $2.05. The company also noted a substantial growth in its user base, ending the quarter with an average of 15.98 million monthly players, reflecting an 11% increase year-over-year. Particularly, its U.S. operations, including FanDuel, displayed rapid scalability and robust performance. FanDuel's U.S. revenue witnessed a notable 17% surge year-over-year, with sportsbook revenue up by 11% and iGaming revenue soaring by 92%. The positive financial results indicate a promising outlook for Flutter Entertainment PLC.
Motorsport Games to announce Q2 2025 financial results on August 13 after market close, followed by a 5pm ET conference call.

data.title: Cipher Pharmaceuticals reported record revenue of $13.4 million in Q2 2025, a 152% increase from Q2 2024. Adjusted EBITDA was $7.6 million, up 148%. Natroba TM sales were $7.8 million, a 16% increase from Q1 2025. Epuris sales grew by 14%. Cash from operations was $6.0 million. $15.0 million debt repayment and $2.1 million share repurchases in Q2. $7.0 million debt repayment post Q2. Total revenue up 152%, gross profit up 159%, EBITDA up 290% in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024. Profit and earnings per share rose as well. Debt repayment led to $18.0 million outstanding. Focus is on Natroba TM growth in the U.S. and global expansion efforts. More details available at www.cipherpharma.com.
data.description: Cipher Pharmaceuticals Inc. reported its highest revenue quarter to date, reaching $13.4 million in Q2 2025. Adjusted EBITDA also surged to $7.6 million, up by 148% from Q2 2024. Epuris sales volumes increased by 14%, while Natroba TM sales hit $7.8 million, up by 16% from Q1 2025. The company saw strong cash generation, with $6.0 million from operations in Q2 2025, leading to significant debt repayments and share repurchases. Cipher's net income rose to $5.9 million in Q2 2025. Natroba TM received preferred status in Illinois Medicaid, with Permethrin 5% being downgraded. The company also repurchased 230,278 common shares during the quarter. Q2 2025 financial review showed total revenue at $13.4 million, a 152% increase from Q2 2024. Total gross profit rose to $10.9 million. EBITDA stood at $8.6 million, up by 290%. Adjusted EBITDA surged to $7.6 million in Q2 2025. Cipher aims to grow Natroba TM market share, obtain regulatory approval in Canada, and expand globally. They also aim to acquire dermatology products to enhance their business. The financial statements are available on Cipher's website, with a conference call scheduled on August 8, 2025, to discuss these results.

data.title: Trump signs executive orders allowing 401(k) plans to include crypto and targets discriminatory banking practices against crypto firms.
data.description: President Donald Trump recently signed two executive orders boosting the presence of digital assets within the U.S. financial system. The first order permits 401(k) retirement plans to incorporate cryptocurrencies, while the second focuses on combating perceived discriminatory banking practices impacting crypto firms. Under the initial order, the Department of Labor is tasked with reviewing its existing guidance, which presently limits exposure to alternative assets in employer-sponsored retirement plans. This step is designed to reassess regulatory interpretations governed by the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA), with additional calls for the Securities and Exchange Commission and Treasury Department to explore potential regulatory changes facilitating broader retail access to digital assets via accredited investor routes. These actions align with previous initiatives by Trump, such as establishing a strategic digital asset reserve and a national effort to... Access the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock market fades despite chip gains, AppLovin and Robinhood signal buys, Sezzle and MP Materials move late.
data.description: Stock market performance was mixed on Thursday despite strong gains in the chip sector. Notably, AppLovin and Robinhood showed positive buy signals amidst the overall decline. Late movers included Sezzle and MP Materials, with Sezzle experiencing a significant decrease. Dow Jones Futures were mentioned in relation to the performance of these companies.

data.title: Stocks mixed: Dow dropped 224 points, Nasdaq gained, Nvidia hits new high. Market update by Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: On Thursday, the stock market experienced a mixed close with major indexes following a volatile pattern. Initially rising, Nvidia reached a new high during the day. The Dow swung significantly to drop 224 points, while the Nasdaq managed to eke out a slight gain. This fluctuation was indicative of the uncertain market conditions prevailing on the day.

data.title: SIR Royalty Income Fund's Q2 2025 financial results show a 8.1% Pooled Revenue growth, $72.9M revenue, and $2.5M distributable cash. For more, visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: The SIR Royalty Income Fund reported its financial results for Q2 2025, ending June 30, 2025. They saw Pooled Revenue reach $72.9 million, an 8.1% increase from Q2 2024. Same store sales grew by 1.1%, with net earnings at $3.5 million. Distributable cash was $2.5 million, with a payout ratio of 94.5%. They closed a location in Longueuil, and opened a new Scaddabush restaurant in Barrie. The Fund also increased its monthly cash distribution to $0.10 per Fund unit. Following a cybersecurity incident in 2024, they received a $2.0 million payout under their cyber insurance policy. The Fund's financial results for the period show continued growth and strategic developments.

data.title: Tree Island Steel's Q2 2025 revenues drop to $42.3M from $54M in 2024 due to lower U.S. sales amid tariffs, with gross profit at $3.9M. Adjusted EBITDA down to $2.2M from $2.9M. Six-month revenues fall to $92.5M from $110.5M in 2024, with gross profit at $7.8M and adjusted EBITDA at $4.3M. US and Canadian tariffs impacting sales strategies, with a focus on profitability and cost control.
data.description: Tree Island Steel reported its financial results for the six months ended June 30, 2025. In the three-month period, revenues decreased by $11.7 million to $42.3 million, with gross profit dropping to $3.9 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $2.2 million, down from $2.9 million year-over-year. For the six months, revenues fell by $18.0 million to $92.5 million, while gross profit declined to $7.8 million. Adjusted EBITDA also decreased to $4.3 million. These drops were attributed to lower U.S. sales volumes due to tariffs and strategic changes. The company aims to enhance profitability by adjusting strategies and controlling costs. Revenue, cost of sales, and gross profit numbers are detailed in the article. Chief Operating Officer Nancy Davies highlighted the fast-changing tariff landscape and the necessity to adapt strategies accordingly.

data.title: Expedia's stock soars on Q2 results, revenue of $3.79 billion, up 6% YoY, beats estimates. EPS at $4.24, beats $3.90 consensus.
data.description: Travel giant Expedia Group Inc announced better than expected financial results for the second quarter, surpassing both analyst estimates and its own forecasts. The company reported revenue of $3.79 billion, a 6% increase from the previous year, outperforming the expected $3.70 billion. Earnings per share stood at $4.24, also beating the consensus estimate of $3.90. Expedia recorded a total of 105.5 million booked room nights, reflecting a 7% growth compared to the previous year. This positive news led to a rise in EXPE stock, with further details available online.

data.title: Wajax Corporation announced 2025 Q2 results: Revenue $547.1M, cash flow $67.4M, leverage ratio improved to 2.35 times. (Note: character count exceeds 100)
data.description: Wajax Corporation reported its 2025 second-quarter results with revenue at $547.1 million, down from $568.3 million in the same quarter last year. The gross profit margin decreased to 19.1% compared to 20.9% in 2024, but improved from the earlier quarter. Cash flow from operations was $67.4 million, up from $35.8 million in Q2 of 2024. Inventory decreased by $56.0 million from the prior quarter and $147.5 million from peak levels in March 2024. Leverage ratio improved to 2.35 times compared to 2.53 in Q1 2025. Adjusted Earnings Per Share (EPS) was $0.77, down from $1.06 in the same quarter last year. Mr. Domagalski mentioned inventory optimization and cost discipline driving robust cash flow. The company is focusing on right-sizing inventory, streamlining costs, and enhancing margins. Revenue in western Canada increased, while central and eastern Canada saw declines. Adjusted EBIT decreased by 25.2% to $29.4 million. The Corporation declared a dividend of $0.35 per share for Q3 2025. Looking ahead, Wajax is optimistic about strong customer demand in mining and energy sectors and remains focused on optimizing inventory and managing costs to generate long-term value and capitalize on opportunities.

data.title: MP Materials (NYSE: MP) stock soars to all-time high after Q2 earnings beat. Revenue: $57.39M (beat $46.31M estimate). Loss: 13 cents/share (beat 18 cents/share estimate). Total revenue up 84% YoY.
data.description: MP Materials Corp (NYSE: MP) released their Q2 financial results, surpassing analyst expectations with reported revenue of $57.39 million, toppling estimates of $46.31 million. The company also disclosed an adjusted loss of 13 cents per share for the quarter, outperforming the anticipated loss of 18 cents per share by analysts. This marks an 84% increase in total revenue compared to the previous year, attributed to elevated production levels of separated products, thus enhancing the overall product mix. MP is currently challenging its 52-week high post this positive financial report. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SoundHound AI (NASDAQ: SOUN) has record-breaking Q2 earnings with quarterly revenue at $42.68 million, beats estimate.
data.description: SoundHound AI, Inc. (NASDAQ: SOUN) unveiled its Q2 results post Thursday's market close. The report showed adjusted losses of $0.03 per share, surpassing the estimated $0.09 loss. Quarterly revenue stood at $42.68 million, exceeding the expected $32.88 million. For real-time updates on SOUN stock price and chart, visit their website. CEO of SoundHound AI urges critics to review the details and learn. Find more insights on this story online.

data.title: Lassonde Industries Q2-2025 results show sales of $742.4m, 10.1% growth. Operating profit at $54.4m, EPS $5.03, a 2.4% increase. Adjusted EBITDA $84.4m, up 13.1%. Total assets $2.32b, total debt $651.1m. Dividend of $1.10 per share paid. Sales growth expected above 10%, monitoring commodity costs and expenses.
data.description: Lassonde Industries Inc. reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2025 with significant growth compared to the same period last year. Sales totaled $742.4 million, up by $117.8 million, driven by market share gains in Canada and continued growth in US brands. Gross profit reached $195.7 million, a $20.0 million increase, with adjusted EBITDA at $84.4 million, up 13.1%. The corporation's profit attributable to shareholders was $34.3 million, resulting in earnings per share of $5.03. Lassonde expects sales growth slightly above 10% for 2025, driven by factors like a full-year impact of recent acquisitions and demand strategies in the US market. Inflation in commodity costs remains a concern, impacting the corporation's expenses and profitability. Total assets stood at $2,324.9 million, with long-term debt at $651.1 million, resulting in a net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio of 2.03:1. Operating activities used $3.1 million in cash, a decrease from the previous year, reflecting changes in working capital items. Lassonde's outlook for fiscal 2025 anticipates ongoing challenges related to consumer financial health, inflation, and market reactions, while closely monitoring commodity costs and expenses to support growth initiatives.

data.title: Esperion grants 73,500 RSUs to 12 employees under NASDAQ Rule, August 5, 2025.
data.description: On August 5, 2025, Esperion (NASDAQ: ESPR) in Ann Arbor, Michigan, disclosed that they awarded 73,500 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 12 newly hired employees as part of Esperion’s 2017 Inducement Equity Incentive Plan.
